topic.en: Comparing Cities · Comparatives and Superlatives · Travel and Places
topic.ja: 都市の比較・比較級と最上級・旅行と場所
topic.fr: Comparer les villes · Comparatifs et superlatifs · Voyages et lieux
topic.ko: 도시 비교 · 비교급과 최상급 · 여행과 장소

core_title.ja: どの都市がいちばんいい？

core_reading_html.en: Many people like to visit a city. Vienna is a beautiful capital with great music. It stands near a river and has a famous museum. Liverpool is a busy port and many people travel there. New Orleans is famous for jazz and a fun festival. Some cities are bigger, but many people think these cities are the best places for a tourist.
core_reading_html.ja: 多くの人は都市を訪れるのが好きです。ウィーンは美しい首都で、すばらしい音楽があります。大きな川の近くにあり、有名な博物館もあります。リヴァプールはにぎやかな港で、多くの人がそこへ旅行します。ニューオーリンズはジャズと楽しい祭りで有名です。もっと大きい都市もありますが、多くの人はこれらの都市が最高の場所であり、観光客に人気だと考えています。
core_reading_html.fr: Beaucoup de personnes aiment visiter une ville. Vienne est une belle capitale avec une grande musique. Elle est près d'une rivière et possède un célèbre musée. Liverpool est un grand port où beaucoup de gens voyagent. La Nouvelle-Orléans est célèbre pour le jazz et un festival populaire. Certaines villes sont plus grandes, mais beaucoup pensent que ces villes sont les meilleures pour un touriste.
core_reading_html.ko: 많은 사람들은 도시를 방문하는 것을 좋아합니다. 빈은 아름다운 수도이며 훌륭한 음악으로 유명합니다. 큰 강 근처에 있으며 유명한 박물관도 있습니다. 리버풀은 중요한 항구이고 많은 사람들이 그곳으로 여행합니다. 뉴올리언스는 재즈와 즐거운 축제로 유명합니다. 더 큰 도시도 있지만 많은 사람들은 이 도시들이 최고의 여행지이며 관광객에게 인기가 있다고 생각합니다.

en.definition: A large town where many people live.
The main city of a country.
Sounds made by instruments or singing.
A large natural stream of water.
A place where historical objects are shown.
A place where ships arrive and leave.
To go from one place to another.
A person visiting a place for pleasure.

ja.definition: 人が多く住む大きな町。
自然に流れる水の流れ。
歴史や文化を展示する場所。

fr.definition: Un grand endroit où vivent beaucoup de personnes.
Des sons créés par des instruments ou des voix.
Un lieu qui présente des objets historiques.
Un lieu où arrivent les bateaux.
Aller d’un endroit à un autre.
Une personne qui voyage pour le plaisir.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Madrid is more expensive than Rome.

script.speaker: B
script.text: No, it isn't. Madrid is much cheaper.

script.speaker: A
script.text: The buildings in Rome are more modern than the buildings in New York.

script.speaker: B
script.text: No, they aren't. They're much older.

script.speaker: A
script.text: The Underground in London is better than the Metro in Paris.

script.speaker: B
script.text: No! The Underground is much worse.

en.title: Bigger and better!
en.label: Conversation 2: Mel's got a better job

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: Why did you leave London? You had a good job.

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: Yes, but I've got a better job here.

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: And you had a big flat in London.

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: Well, I've got a bigger flat here. Three bedrooms and a garden.

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: But you haven't got any friends!

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: People are much friendlier here. And the country isn't boring - it's much more exciting than London.

script.speaker: A
script.text: That house is very big.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, it's the biggest house in the village.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Castle Combe is a very pretty village.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, it's the prettiest village in England.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Anna is a very intelligent student.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Yes, she's the most intelligent student in the class.

en.title: Bigger and better!
en.label: Listening 1: A drive in the country

script.speaker: Driver
script.text: I drove out of the garage, along the road, and under the bridge.

script.speaker: Driver
script.text: Then I drove past the pub, up the hill, and down the hill.

script.speaker: Driver
script.text: But then I drove over the river.

script.speaker: Driver
script.text: And then - it was terrible - I went through the hedge, and into the lake!

script.speaker: A
script.text: マドリードはローマより物価が高いよね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: ううん、違うよ。マドリードのほうがずっと安い。

script.speaker: A
script.text: ローマの建物はニューヨークより近代的だね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: いや、そうじゃない。ずっと古いよ。

script.speaker: A
script.text: ロンドンの地下鉄はパリのメトロより良いよね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: 違う！ロンドンの地下鉄のほうがずっと悪いよ。

ja.title: より大きく、より良く！
ja.label: 会話 2: メルのより良い仕事

script.speaker: タラ
script.text: なんでロンドンやめたの？良い仕事だったじゃん。

script.speaker: メル
script.text: うん、でもこっちのほうがもっと良い仕事なんだ。

script.speaker: タラ
script.text: ロンドンには広いマンションあったでしょ。

script.speaker: メル
script.text: それが、こっちのほうがもっと広いんだ。寝室3つに庭つき。

script.speaker: タラ
script.text: でも友達いないんでしょ！

script.speaker: メル
script.text: こっちの人のほうがずっと親切だよ。それに田舎は退屈なんかじゃない。ロンドンよりずっと刺激的。

ja.title: より大きく、より良く！
ja.label: 会話 3: 一番大きくて最高

script.speaker: A
script.text: あの家、とても大きいね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: うん、この村で一番大きい家だよ。

script.speaker: A
script.text: キャッスル・コンブはとてもきれいな村ですね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: ええ、イギリスで一番きれいな村です。

script.speaker: A
script.text: アンナはとても頭が良い学生だね。

script.speaker: B
script.text: うん、クラスで一番頭が良い学生だよ。

ja.title: より大きく、より良く！
ja.label: リスニング 1: 田舎のドライブ

script.speaker: 運転手
script.text: ガレージを出て、道に沿って走り、橋の下をくぐりました。

script.speaker: 運転手
script.text: それからパブを通り過ぎて、坂を上がり、坂を下りました。

script.speaker: 運転手
script.text: でもそのあと、川を渡ってしまって。

script.speaker: 運転手
script.text: それから――最悪でした――生垣を突き破って、湖に落ちちゃいました！

script.speaker: A
script.text: Madrid est plus chère que Rome.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Non, pas du tout. Madrid est beaucoup moins chère.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Les immeubles à Rome sont plus modernes qu'à New York.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Non, ils ne le sont pas. Ils sont beaucoup plus vieux.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Le métro de Londres est meilleur que celui de Paris.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Non ! Le métro de Londres est bien pire.

fr.title: Plus grand et meilleur !
fr.label: Conversation 2 : Mel a un meilleur travail

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: Pourquoi as-tu quitté Londres ? Tu avais un bon travail.

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: Oui, mais j'ai un meilleur travail ici.

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: Et tu avais un grand appartement à Londres.

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: Eh bien, j'ai un appartement plus grand ici. Trois chambres et un jardin.

script.speaker: Tara
script.text: Mais tu n'as pas d'amis !

script.speaker: Mel
script.text: Les gens sont bien plus sympathiques ici. Et la campagne n'est pas ennuyeuse - c'est bien plus excitant que Londres.

fr.title: Plus grand et meilleur !
fr.label: Conversation 3 : Le plus grand et le meilleur

script.speaker: A
script.text: Cette maison est très grande.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Oui, c'est la plus grande maison du village.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Castle Combe est un très joli village.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Oui, c'est le plus joli village d'Angleterre.

script.speaker: A
script.text: Anna est une étudiante très intelligente.

script.speaker: B
script.text: Oui, c'est l'étudiante la plus intelligente de la classe.

fr.title: Plus grand et meilleur !
fr.label: Compréhension orale 1 : Une promenade en campagne

script.speaker: Conducteur
script.text: Je suis sorti du garage, j'ai longé la route, et je suis passé sous le pont.

script.speaker: Conducteur
script.text: Ensuite, j'ai dépassé le pub, j'ai monté la côte, et j'ai descendu la côte.

script.speaker: Conducteur
script.text: Mais après, j'ai traversé la rivière.

script.speaker: Conducteur
script.text: Et puis - c'était terrible - j'ai traversé la haie, et je suis tombé dans le lac !

script.speaker: A
script.text: 마드리드는 로마보다 물가가 더 비싸.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 아니, 그렇지 않아. 마드리드가 훨씬 더 싸.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 로마의 건물들은 뉴욕의 건물들보다 더 현대적이야.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 아니, 그렇지 않아. 훨씬 더 오래됐어.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 런던 지하철이 파리 메트로보다 더 좋아.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 아니! 런던 지하철이 훨씬 더 나빠.

script.speaker: 타라
script.text: 런던은 왜 그만뒀어? 좋은 직장 있었잖아.

script.speaker: 멜
script.text: 응, 그런데 여기가 더 나은 직장이야.

script.speaker: 타라
script.text: 런던에 넓은 아파트도 있었고.

script.speaker: 멜
script.text: 음, 여기가 더 넓은 아파트야. 침실 세 개에 정원도 있어.

script.speaker: 타라
script.text: 그런데 친구는 한 명도 없잖아!

script.speaker: 멜
script.text: 여기 사람들이 훨씬 더 친절해. 그리고 시골은 지루하지 않아 - 런던보다 훨씬 더 신나.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 저 집은 매우 크다.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 맞아, 마을에서 가장 큰 집이야.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 캐슬 콤은 매우 아름다운 마을이야.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 맞아, 잉글랜드에서 가장 아름다운 마을이지.

script.speaker: A
script.text: 안나는 매우 똑똑한 학생이야.

script.speaker: B
script.text: 응, 반에서 가장 똑똑한 학생이야.

script.speaker: 운전자
script.text: 나는 차고를 나와서 길을 따라 쭉 가다가 다리 밑을 지나갔습니다.

script.speaker: 운전자
script.text: 그리고 나서 술집을 지나쳐 언덕을 올라갔다가 언덕을 내려왔습니다.

script.speaker: 운전자
script.text: 그런데 그 다음에 강을 건너고 말았어요.

script.speaker: 운전자
script.text: 그리고 나서 - 정말 끔찍했어요 - 울타리를 뚫고 호수에 빠져버렸습니다!

extra_reading_html.en:
Vienna is the capital of Austria. It stands on the River Danube. Its music, theatre, museums, and parks make it a popular tourist centre. Its university opened in 1365. Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven, and Schubert all came to work here. It is the home of the Vienna Philharmonic orchestra.

Liverpool is Britain's second biggest port, after London. It stands on the River Mersey. It is an important passenger port for travel to Ireland. King John named Liverpool in 1207. Liverpool's most famous musicians are the Beatles. They started the group there in 1959.

New Orleans stands on the banks of the Mississippi River. It became an important trade centre for sugar, spices, and slaves. Many Irish immigrants live there. It is famous for jazz music and the annual Mardi Gras carnival.
extra_reading_html.ja:
ウィーンはオーストリアの首都で、ドナウ川沿いにあります。音楽、劇場、美術館、公園が多く、人気の観光地です。1365年に大学が開校し、ハイドン、モーツァルト、ベートーヴェン、シューベルトなどが活躍しました。ウィーン・フィルハーモニー管弦楽団の本拠地でもあります。

リヴァプールはロンドンに次ぐイギリスで2番目に大きな港町で、マージー川沿いにあります。アイルランドへの重要な旅客港です。1207年にジョン王がリヴァプールと命名しました。リヴァプールで最も有名な音楽家はビートルズで、1959年にこの地でグループを結成しました。

ニューオーリンズはミシシッピ川のほとりに位置しています。かつては砂糖、香辛料、奴隷貿易の重要な中心地でした。多くのアイルランド移民が住んでいます。ジャズ音楽と毎年恒例のマルディグラ・カーニバルで有名です。
extra_reading_html.fr:
Vienne est la capitale de l'Autriche. Elle est située sur le Danube. Sa musique, ses théâtres, ses musées et ses parcs en font un centre touristique populaire. Son université a été ouverte en 1365. Haydn, Mozart, Beethoven et Schubert y ont tous travaillé. C'est le foyer de l'Orchestre philharmonique de Vienne.

Liverpool est le deuxième plus grand port de Grande-Bretagne, après Londres. Elle se trouve sur la rivière Mersey. C'est un port passager important pour voyager vers l'Irlande. Le roi Jean a nommé Liverpool en 1207. Les musiciens les plus célèbres de Liverpool sont les Beatles, qui ont formé le groupe là-bas en 1959.

La Nouvelle-Orléans se trouve sur les rives du fleuve Mississippi. Elle est devenue un centre commercial important pour le sucre, les épices et les esclaves. Beaucoup d'immigrants irlandais y vivent. Elle est célèbre pour sa musique jazz et son carnaval annuel de Mardi Gras.
extra_reading_html.ko:
빈은 오스트리아의 수도로, 다뉴브 강변에 위치해 있습니다. 음악, 극장, 박물관, 공원 덕분에 인기 있는 관광지입니다. 1365년에 대학이 설립되었으며, 하이든, 모차르트, 베토벤, 슈베르트가 모두 이곳에서 활동했습니다. 빈 필하모닉 오케스트라의 본거지이기도 합니다.

리버풀은 런던 다음으로 영국에서 두 번째로 큰 항구 도시이며, 머지 강변에 있습니다. 아일랜드로 가는 중요한 여객항입니다. 1207년 존 왕이 리버풀이라는 이름을 붙였습니다. 리버풀에서 가장 유명한 음악가는 비틀즈이며, 그들은 1959년 이곳에서 그룹을 결성했습니다.

뉴올리언스는 미시시피 강둑에 위치해 있습니다. 이곳은 설탕, 향신료, 노예 무역의 중요한 중심지가 되었습니다. 많은 아일랜드계 이민자들이 거주하고 있습니다. 재즈 음악과 매년 열리는 마디 그라 카니발로 유명합니다.

en.question: Vienna's university was established in the 14th century.
Liverpool is the biggest port in Britain.
en.explanation: False. It is the second biggest port after London.

en.question: The Beatles started their music group in New Orleans.
en.explanation: False. The Beatles started in Liverpool.

en.question: New Orleans is famous for classical music.
en.explanation: False. New Orleans is famous for jazz music.

en.question: The Mississippi River flows through New Orleans.
en.explanation: True. The city stands on the banks of the Mississippi River.

ja.question: ウィーン大学は14世紀に開校した。
ja.explanation: 真実です。1365年に開校しました。

ja.question: リヴァプールはイギリス最大の港である。
ja.explanation: 誤りです。ロンドンに次ぐ2番目に大きな港です。

ja.question: ビートルズはニューオーリンズで結成された。
ja.explanation: 誤りです。ビートルズはリヴァプールで結成されました。

ja.question: ニューオーリンズはクラシック音楽で有名である。
ja.explanation: 誤りです。ニューオーリンズはジャズで有名です。

ja.question: ニューオーリンズはミシシッピ川沿いにある。
ja.explanation: 真実です。ミシシッピ川のほとりに位置しています。

fr.question: L'université de Vienne a ouvert ses portes au 14ème siècle.
Liverpool est le plus grand port de Grande-Bretagne.
fr.explanation: Faux. C'est le deuxième après Londres.

fr.question: Les Beatles ont formé leur groupe à la Nouvelle-Orléans.
fr.explanation: Faux. Les Beatles ont commencé à Liverpool.

fr.question: La Nouvelle-Orléans est célèbre pour la musique classique.
fr.explanation: Faux. La ville est célèbre pour le jazz.

fr.question: La Nouvelle-Orléans se trouve sur les rives du Mississippi.
fr.explanation: Vrai. C'est précisé dans le texte.

ko.question: 리버풀은 영국에서 가장 큰 항구이다.
ko.explanation: 거짓입니다. 런던 다음으로 두 번째로 큰 항구입니다.

en.text: The city is ... than the country.
I think it's safer in the country.
The city is more exciting than the country. I think it's safer in the country.
I've got a big flat. It's got a garden.
Claridge's is the oldest hotel. The Plaza is the biggest.

ja.text: 田舎の方が安全だと思います。
都会は田舎より刺激的です。 田舎の方が安全だと思います。
私には広いアパートがあります。 それには庭があります。
最も大きい／最も高価な
クラリッジズは最も古いホテルです。 プラザは最も大きいです。

fr.text: La ville est ... que la campagne.
Je pense que c'est plus sûr à la campagne.
La ville est plus excitante que la campagne. Je pense que c'est plus sûr à la campagne.
J'ai un grand appartement. Il a un jardin.
le plus grand / le plus cher
le moins cher / le plus ancien
Claridge's est l'hôtel le plus ancien. Le Plaza est le plus grand.

ko.text: 도시는 시골보다 더 흥미진진합니다. 시골이 더 안전하다고 생각해요.
저는 넓은 아파트를 가지고 있어요. 그것은 정원을 가지고 있어요.
Claridge's가 가장 오래된 호텔입니다. The Plaza가 가장 큽니다.

grammar_html.en:
1. Comparative adjectives

We use comparative adjectives to compare two people, places, or things.

  • • For short adjectives (1 syllable): add -er → old → older, big → bigger
  • • For adjectives ending in -y: change y to i and add -er → noisy → noisier, dirty → dirtier
  • • For long adjectives (2+ syllables): use 'more' + adjective → expensive → more expensive, exciting → more exciting
  • • Irregular: good → better, bad → worse, far → further/farther

2. Superlative adjectives

We use superlative adjectives to compare three or more things. Use 'the' before the superlative.

  • • Short adjectives: add -est → the oldest, the biggest
  • • Adjectives ending in -y: change y to i and add -est → the noisiest, the dirtiest
  • • Long adjectives: use 'the most' + adjective → the most expensive, the most exciting

3. have got

We use 'have got' to express possession in spoken British English.

  • • Positive: I/You/We/They have got (I've got) / He/She/It has got (He's got)
  • • Negative: I haven't got / He hasn't got
  • • Question: Have you got? / Has she got?
  • • Past tense: had (no 'got')

content_check_question.en: This unit teaches how to compare city and country life, use 'have got' to talk about possessions, and use superlatives to describe the best/worst hotels. How do you form comparative and superlative adjectives? What is the difference between 'have' and 'have got'? How do you say 'good' and 'bad' in comparative and superlative forms?

content_check_keywords.en: comparatives, superlatives, have got, city vs country, directions, hotels

content_check_placeholder.en: Explain how to compare two things using -er/more, how to use have got to show possession, and how to use the + -est/most for superlatives...

deepening_question.en: The unit compares city and country life and features three musical cities: Vienna, Liverpool, and New Orleans. Do you prefer living in a city or in the countryside? Why? Which of the three musical cities would you most like to visit and why? What kind of music is your city or country famous for?

deepening_placeholder.en: I prefer living in the city because... / I prefer the countryside because... I would like to visit... because...

critical_question.en: In this unit, you learned to compare places and use 'have got' to describe possessions. Why are comparative and superlative forms important when describing hotels, cities, or travel experiences? How could you use these forms to recommend a hotel or a city to a friend?

critical_placeholder.en: Comparatives and superlatives help us compare and recommend because... I would tell my friend: Hotel X is cheaper than Hotel Y, but Hotel Z is the most comfortable...
